evals.name: add-global-dev-guidelines-detected-languages
evals.prompt: Add a development-guidelines page to the spec at docs/specs/. The repo is a monorepo with a Rust backend (Cargo.toml, rust-toolchain.toml) and a TypeScript frontend (tsconfig.json, pnpm-lock.yaml). Use Tiger Style.
evals.expected_output: A new global spec page at docs/specs/development-guidelines.md scoped Repo-wide, with the Status/Date/Owner/Scope header and the closing Assumptions/Decisions/Open questions block. The page detects Rust and TypeScript, confirms them, and carries one Toolchain table covering both, a 'Tiger Style — the pervasive style' section, a Defensive coding section with both an 'Assertions in Rust' and an 'Assertions in TypeScript' subsection, a Limits and bounds meta-rule (no concrete values — those are per-app), a Version control section matching the repo's VCS, a 'Rust conventions' and a 'TypeScript conventions' section, repository hygiene, AI-agent guidelines, and a definition of done naming both languages' tooling. docs/README.md is updated to index the new global page. No MVP framing, no marketing words, no emoji.

evals.name: greenfield-ask-language-python
evals.prompt: We're specing a new Python service before any code exists. Add the development-guidelines page to docs/specs/ in Tiger Style.
evals.expected_output: Because nothing is detectable, the skill asks which language(s) to target and the user picks Python. A docs/specs/development-guidelines.md page is produced with a Python conventions section that respects the CPython '-O strips assert' caveat (an unconditional invariant helper, not bare assert, for production invariants), a strict type-checker requirement, validation at boundaries via a schema library, and pytest-based testing. Any tool named in the discipline but not yet wired up (no code exists yet) is captured in Open questions rather than asserted in the body. Standard header and closing block; docs/README.md indexed.

evals.name: per-app-delta-pointer
evals.prompt: The global development guidelines at docs/specs/development-guidelines.md already exist for a TypeScript repo. The apps/worker app additionally uses Rust and runs a stricter per-instance concurrency limit. Add a per-app development page for it.
evals.expected_output: A thin per-app page (e.g. docs/worker/specs/NN-development.md) that opens with a 'Read first' pointer to docs/specs/development-guidelines.md and documents ONLY the deltas: the Rust conventions/assertions/toolchain rows and the app-specific concurrency limit value. It does not restate the global TypeScript rules. docs/worker/README.md points at the global specs then lists the per-app set; docs/README.md indexes the per-app spec set. Standard header (with Scope) and closing block.
